mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which forces equity valuations higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in at least 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization bottlenecks that precede inflationary shocks and central bank tightening, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of greater than 0.5 percent fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary policy normalization, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth surpasses 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates signals labor market tightening that supports wage growth and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero information coefficient over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth surpassing 2.0 percent confirms robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating CPI inflation during a tightening cycle force discount rates higher and compress equity valuations, generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL tightening signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 60 percent of tightening regime observations.
